findings.description: The PDF uses a compact table layout with colored header row and alternating row styling, while the HTML renders as a more spacious table with significantly more vertical spacing between rows.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a dense table fitting all 53+ rows on one page; HTML spreads the same content over a much longer scrollable page with generous row padding.

findings.description: The colored header row styling differs - PDF has green/blue colored text in headers while HTML uses plain dark text headers.
findings.evidence: PDF header row has green 'State' text, blue 'Official Fiscal Year 2019' and red 'Fiscal Year 2022 Cumulative' text; HTML headers are styled in muted dark text without the same color coding.

findings.description: North Carolina row is highlighted in red/bold in the PDF but appears as a regular row in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'North Carolina' row (rank 39) with red text highlighting; HTML shows it as a normal row without special formatting.

findings.description: USDA logo appears in both versions but styling differs slightly - PDF has it with a green line underneath, HTML has a similar but simpler rendering.
findings.evidence: Both show USDA logo at top left with 'United States Department of Agriculture' text; minor visual styling differences.

findings.description: The state name 'District of Columbia' is truncated to 'District of' in the HTML, matching the PDF's truncated display but missing the full name.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'District of' (truncated in the table cell), HTML also shows 'District of'. Both are truncated identically.

findings.description: The page header area includes the USDA branding and 'United States Department of Agriculture' text, and then the full table content appears within a nested <main> element, but there is no actual content duplication of table data.
findings.evidence: The HTML has two <main> elements nested, which is a structural issue but does not cause content duplication.

findings.description: Oklahoma's Official Fiscal Year 2019 Payment Error Rate is listed as 8.09 in the HTML but the PDF shows 8.09. Wait - checking again: PDF shows 8.09 for Oklahoma. HTML shows 8.09. Actually these match. Let me recheck all values more carefully.
findings.evidence: After careful re-examination, all 53 state/territory rows plus the U.S. Total row have matching values across all four data columns between the PDF and HTML. No data integrity issues found.

findings.description: The USDA logo image has alt text 'USDA logo' which is reasonable and accurate for the USDA seal/logo shown in the PDF header.
